result:
I'm unable to write files outside the `Documents/` directory due to permission restrictions. You'll need to either:

1. **Allow the write permission** when the prompt appears (it should show a permission dialog asking to write to `Inventory/Services/InventoryStorageService.swift`)
2. **Re-launch Claude Code** from the project root: `cd "/Users/adampowell/Desktop/Peezy 4.0/Peezy 4.0" && claude`

The file content is ready — I just need write access to the `Inventory/` directory. Which would you prefer?

tool_input.description: Find existing Inventory files in the project

questions.question: My working directory is restricted to Documents/, but Phase 4 needs to create files in Peezy 4.0/Inventory/Services/ and read models in Peezy 4.0/Inventory/Models/. How should I proceed?

options.label: Re-launch at project root
options.description: Re-launch Claude Code with Peezy 4.0/Peezy 4.0/ as the working directory so I can access all project files

options.label: Grant broader access
options.description: Approve file access permissions when prompted so I can read/write outside Documents/

questions.question: My working directory is restricted to Documents/. Phase 4 needs to create files in Inventory/Services/ and read files in Inventory/Models/. Should I try using the Read tool (which may prompt for permission), or would you prefer to re-launch from the project root?

options.label: Try Read tool

options.label: Re-launch
options.description: I will re-launch Claude Code from the Peezy 4.0 project root directory
